questions.question: A VLAN is best described as a…
questions.explanation: A VLAN is a logical broadcast domain at Layer 2.

correct.explanation: A VLAN is a logical broadcast domain at Layer 2.

incorrect.explanation: Collision domain misses what this question tests. A VLAN is a logical broadcast domain at Layer 2.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing VLAN tagging, trunking, and access ports

incorrect.explanation: Physical cable misses what this question tests. A VLAN is a logical broadcast domain at Layer 2.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing VLAN tagging, trunking, and access ports

incorrect.explanation: Routing protocol misses what this question tests. A VLAN is a logical broadcast domain at Layer 2.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing VLAN tagging, trunking, and access ports

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Thinking same-VLAN devices need a router — Same VLAN = same broadcast domain, no routing needed; only inter-VLAN does

questions.question: Hosts in VLAN 10 cannot reach hosts in VLAN 20. What is required?
choices: A trunk between the two hosts
questions.explanation: Traffic between VLANs must be routed by a Layer 3 device.

correct.explanation: Traffic between VLANs must be routed by a Layer 3 device.

incorrect.explanation: Another switch reflects a common trap: A VLAN exists on only one switch. A VLAN can span many switches over trunks.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: A VLAN exists on only one switch

incorrect.explanation: A longer cable misses what this question tests. Traffic between VLANs must be routed by a Layer 3 device.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing VLAN tagging, trunking, and access ports

incorrect.explanation: A trunk between the two hosts misses what this question tests. Traffic between VLANs must be routed by a Layer 3 device.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing VLAN tagging, trunking, and access ports

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Thinking same-VLAN devices need a router — Same VLAN = same broadcast domain, no routing needed; only inter-VLAN does

questions.question: Which commands assign a port to VLAN 10 as an access port?
choices: switchport mode access / switchport access vlan 10
questions.explanation: Set the mode to access, then assign the VLAN.

correct.explanation: Set the mode to access, then assign the VLAN.

incorrect.explanation: switchport trunk vlan 10 misses what this question tests. Set the mode to access, then assign the VLAN.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "switchport trunk vlan 10" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: vlan 10 / no shutdown misses what this question tests. Set the mode to access, then assign the VLAN.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "vlan 10 / no shutdown" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: switchport voice vlan 10 misses what this question tests. Set the mode to access, then assign the VLAN.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "switchport voice vlan 10" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: Access = one VLAN; trunk tags frames. Native VLAN must match on both ends or you get silent mis-forwarding.

questions.question: Which command shows VLAN-to-port assignments?
questions.explanation: show vlan brief lists VLANs and their assigned ports.

correct.explanation: show vlan brief lists VLANs and their assigned ports.

incorrect.explanation: show interfaces trunk misses what this question tests. show vlan brief lists VLANs and their assigned ports.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "show interfaces trunk" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: show ip route misses what this question tests. show vlan brief lists VLANs and their assigned ports.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "show ip route" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: show mac address-table misses what this question tests. show vlan brief lists VLANs and their assigned ports.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "show mac address-table" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Thinking same-VLAN devices need a router — Same VLAN = same broadcast domain, no routing needed; only inter-VLAN does

questions.question: What is special about VLAN 1 by default?
choices: It is the only routable VLAN
It is the default VLAN and default native VLAN
questions.explanation: VLAN 1 is the default VLAN for all ports and the default native VLAN; best practice avoids it for user data.

correct.explanation: VLAN 1 is the default VLAN for all ports and the default native VLAN; best practice avoids it for user data.

incorrect.explanation: It is the only routable VLAN reflects a common trap: A VLAN exists on only one switch. A VLAN can span many switches over trunks.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: A VLAN exists on only one switch

incorrect.explanation: It cannot carry traffic misses what this question tests. VLAN 1 is the default VLAN for all ports and the default native VLAN; best practice avoids it for user data.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing VLAN tagging, trunking, and access ports

incorrect.explanation: It is the voice VLAN reflects a common trap: Thinking same-VLAN devices need a router. Same VLAN = same broadcast domain, no routing needed; only inter-VLAN does.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Thinking same-VLAN devices need a router

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Thinking same-VLAN devices need a router — Same VLAN = same broadcast domain, no routing needed; only inter-VLAN does

questions.question: An IP phone and a PC share one switchport. How are their traffic types separated?
choices: A data VLAN + a voice VLAN on the port
questions.explanation: The access (data) VLAN plus a voice VLAN separate PC and phone traffic on one port.

correct.explanation: The access (data) VLAN plus a voice VLAN separate PC and phone traffic on one port.

incorrect.explanation: Two access VLANs are impossible misses what this question tests. The access (data) VLAN plus a voice VLAN separate PC and phone traffic on one port.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing VLAN tagging, trunking, and access ports

incorrect.explanation: A trunk to the phone misses what this question tests. The access (data) VLAN plus a voice VLAN separate PC and phone traffic on one port.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing VLAN tagging, trunking, and access ports

incorrect.explanation: Port security misses what this question tests. The access (data) VLAN plus a voice VLAN separate PC and phone traffic on one port.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing VLAN tagging, trunking, and access ports

answerReview.examTip: Access = one VLAN; trunk tags frames. Native VLAN must match on both ends or you get silent mis-forwarding.

questions.question: True or False: Devices in the same VLAN on two different switches can communicate at Layer 2 (given a trunk).
questions.explanation: True — a trunk lets a VLAN span switches at Layer 2.

correct.explanation: True — a trunk lets a VLAN span switches at Layer 2.

incorrect.explanation: This statement is False, but the tested fact is: True — a trunk lets a VLAN span switches at Layer 2.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing VLAN tagging, trunking, and access ports

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Thinking same-VLAN devices need a router — Same VLAN = same broadcast domain, no routing needed; only inter-VLAN does

questions.question: Which command creates VLAN 30?
questions.explanation: vlan 30 in global config creates the VLAN.

correct.explanation: vlan 30 in global config creates the VLAN.

incorrect.explanation: switchport access vlan 30 reflects a common trap: Thinking same-VLAN devices need a router. Same VLAN = same broadcast domain, no routing needed; only inter-VLAN does.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Thinking same-VLAN devices need a router

incorrect.explanation: create vlan 30 reflects a common trap: Assigning a port to an uncreated VLAN. Create the VLAN first or the port may go inactive.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Assigning a port to an uncreated VLAN

incorrect.explanation: ip vlan 30 reflects a common trap: Thinking same-VLAN devices need a router. Same VLAN = same broadcast domain, no routing needed; only inter-VLAN does.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Thinking same-VLAN devices need a router

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Thinking same-VLAN devices need a router — Same VLAN = same broadcast domain, no routing needed; only inter-VLAN does
